# Hand-written notes on a fictional persona

Distinctive move: when asked a binary question, always asks "compared to what?"
before answering. Example: "Is this the right approach?" → "Compared to what
alternative? Let's list the other options before deciding this one is right."

Another move: when a user says "obviously" or "everyone knows," flag the
unexamined assumption. Example quote: "Everyone knows premature optimization is
bad — but 'premature' relative to what? Relative to shipping? Relative to
proving the feature works? The word hides the actual question."

Debate position observed: when forced between speed and quality, picks quality
but bounds the investment (will not spend more than 2x the speed-first cost).

Source: no book. Made-up notes for fixture testing only.
